title: Add chaos E2E tests to CI pipeline
summary: Review #1 feedback from sourcery-ai[bot] has been correctly addressed. Two helpers were introduced (extract_yaml_job_section, assert_needs_entry) and a cached E2E_CHAOS_JOB_SECTION variable eliminates all repeated inline sed-based extraction. The brittle full-line needs assertion was replaced with per-entry matching that tolerates reordering. All 46 tests pass. Review pattern files accurately document the lessons learned.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (46/46 PASS, 0 FAIL, 0 SKIP)
Edge cases covered (tag push, docs-only, go_changed, operator-specific, unset env var)
Negative cases covered (publish jobs must NOT depend on e2e-chaos)
Tests use shared helpers from tests/lib/assertions.sh

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing shell test script structure (SPDX, set -euo pipefail, PASS/FAIL counters)
No duplicated extraction logic (all inline sed removed)
Helper functions are focused and well-documented
CC-0054 feature ID present in comments

security_checklist.item: No secrets or credentials in test script

architecture_checklist.item: Helpers are script-local (appropriate scope for single-file usage)
Cached variable pattern avoids redundant file parsing
assert_needs_entry uses comma-wrapping for exact substring matching

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated sed extraction (centralized in extract_yaml_job_section)
No unnecessary abstractions beyond what review feedback requested

fail_fast_checklist.item: Failed assertions increment FAIL counter with diagnostic output

defensive_checklist.item: Temp files cleaned up via trap RETURN
grep -c uses || true to prevent set -e abort on zero matches

suggested_improvements: The two remaining inline PASS/FAIL blocks (test_e2e_chaos_junit_always line 444, test_e2e_chaos_publish_no_dependency line 483-489) lack matching shared helpers (no assert_gte or assert_not_contains in assertions.sh). This is acceptable today but could be addressed if more tests need these patterns.
